catalyst.llm generation: urchinlike W 18 O 49 g-C 3 N 4 composite
catalyst.source: In this work, an urchinlike W 18 O 49 g-C 3 N 4 composite was fabricated via a simple hydrothermal process.
catalyst.context: In this work, an urchinlike W 18 O 49 g-C 3 N 4 composite was fabricated via a simple hydrothermal process.The photocatalytic CO 2 conversion reaction was applied to evaluate the photocatalytic behavior of this catalytic system, and the WOCN-20 wt heterojunction displayed superior photocatalytic behavior under full spectrum light irradiation, which was 6.46 molh -1 g -1 for CO and 3.97 molh -1 g -1 for CH 4 , respectively.Furthermore, in the near-infrared region, the composite photocatalysts also displayed excellent photocatalytic performance.A large number of characterizations and testing measures were carried out to study the components, morphology, and physicochemical properties of W 18 O 49 g-C 3 N 4 photocatalyst, which were helpful to exploring and understanding the reasons for the improvement of catalytic performance.The boosted activity resulted from the tight contact between W 18 O 49 and g-C 3 N 4 and the formation of a Z-scheme heterojunction.The local surface plasmonic resonance (LSPR) of W 5 also contributed to the realization of efficient photocatalysis under near-infrared light.The Z-scheme electron transport model could realize the efficient separation of light-induced e --h pairs and reduce the recombination rate, thereby boosting the CO 2 photocatalytic reduction process.

catalyst.source: The photocatalytic CO 2 conversion reaction was applied to evaluate the photocatalytic behavior of this catalytic system, and the WOCN-20 wt heterojunction displayed superior photocatalytic behavior under full spectrum light irradiation, which was 6.46 molh -1 g -1 for CO and 3.97 molh -1 g -1 for CH 4 , respectively.
catalyst.context: In this work, an urchinlike W 18 O 49 g-C 3 N 4 composite was fabricated via a simple hydrothermal process.The photocatalytic CO 2 conversion reaction was applied to evaluate the photocatalytic behavior of this catalytic system, and the WOCN-20 wt heterojunction displayed superior photocatalytic behavior under full spectrum light irradiation, which was 6.46 molh -1 g -1 for CO and 3.97 molh -1 g -1 for CH 4 , respectively.Furthermore, in the near-infrared region, the composite photocatalysts also displayed excellent photocatalytic performance.A large number of characterizations and testing measures were carried out to study the components, morphology, and physicochemical properties of W 18 O 49 g-C 3 N 4 photocatalyst, which were helpful to exploring and understanding the reasons for the improvement of catalytic performance.The boosted activity resulted from the tight contact between W 18 O 49 and g-C 3 N 4 and the formation of a Z-scheme heterojunction.The local surface plasmonic resonance (LSPR) of W 5 also contributed to the realization of efficient photocatalysis under near-infrared light.The Z-scheme electron transport model could realize the efficient separation of light-induced e --h pairs and reduce the recombination rate, thereby boosting the CO 2 photocatalytic reduction process.

catalyst.source: The strategy mentioned in the previous work was adopted to prepare bare g-C 3 N 4 .
catalyst.context: The information on a detailed description of the characterization techniques, photoelectrochemical measurements, and photocatalytic activity test applied in this work is given in the Supporting Information. 2.1.Chemicals.In this work, tungsten hexachloride (WCl 6 ), melamine (C 3 H 6 N 6 ), and absolute ethanol (C 2 H 5 OH) were purchased from Sinopharm Chemical Reagent Corp., China, and employed without any purification. 2.2.Sample Synthesis.The strategy mentioned in the previous work was adopted to prepare bare g-C 3 N 4 . 40A certain amount of melamine was calcined in a muffle furnace at 550 C for 4 h with a rate of 5 Cmin under an Ar atmosphere.A light-yellow powder was collected by cooling to room temperature and grinding. W 18 O 49 was synthesized by adding WCl 6 to 50 mL of ethanol and stirring for 10 min.Then, the solution was transferred to a Teflonlined autoclave and heated at 200 C for 24 h.The white solid was obtained by centrifugation and washed with DI water and ethanol, followed by drying at 80 C.

catalyst.source: W 18 O 49 was synthesized by adding WCl 6 to 50 mL of ethanol and stirring for 10 min.

catalyst.source: In this work, urchinlike W 18 O 49 was prepared through a hydrothermal strategy and combined with g-C 3 N 4 to form composite photocatalysts.

light source.source: The photocatalytic CO 2 conversion reaction was applied to evaluate the photocatalytic behavior of this catalytic system, and the WOCN-20 wt heterojunction displayed superior photocatalytic behavior under full spectrum light irradiation, which was 6.46 molh -1 g -1 for CO and 3.97 molh -1 g -1 for CH 4 , respectively.
light source.context: In this work, an urchinlike W 18 O 49 g-C 3 N 4 composite was fabricated via a simple hydrothermal process.The photocatalytic CO 2 conversion reaction was applied to evaluate the photocatalytic behavior of this catalytic system, and the WOCN-20 wt heterojunction displayed superior photocatalytic behavior under full spectrum light irradiation, which was 6.46 molh -1 g -1 for CO and 3.97 molh -1 g -1 for CH 4 , respectively.Furthermore, in the near-infrared region, the composite photocatalysts also displayed excellent photocatalytic performance.A large number of characterizations and testing measures were carried out to study the components, morphology, and physicochemical properties of W 18 O 49 g-C 3 N 4 photocatalyst, which were helpful to exploring and understanding the reasons for the improvement of catalytic performance.The boosted activity resulted from the tight contact between W 18 O 49 and g-C 3 N 4 and the formation of a Z-scheme heterojunction.The local surface plasmonic resonance (LSPR) of W 5 also contributed to the realization of efficient photocatalysis under near-infrared light.The Z-scheme electron transport model could realize the efficient separation of light-induced e --h pairs and reduce the recombination rate, thereby boosting the CO 2 photocatalytic reduction process.

light source.source: The local surface plasmonic resonance (LSPR) of W 5 also contributed to the realization of efficient photocatalysis under near-infrared light.
